redLines: Do not invest in speculative US software/technology stocks

Do not touch a running (already successful) system
preferredSectors: Consumer Staples (e.g., Nestlé, Procter & Gamble, Walmart, Costco)
avoidedSectors: Speculative US software and AI/tech mega‑caps

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: Multi‑generational, focused on preserving wealth for grandchildren and the family foundation.
investmentBehavior.liquidity: Low immediate liquidity needs; cash drawn only for pre‑planned expenses (e.g., home renovation, bond coupon sweeps).
investmentBehavior.mandate: Conservative Defensive Global Equity & High‑Grade Fixed Income Mandate

lifeEvents.description: CHF 150,000 withdrawn from cash accounts for a structural renovation of the holiday home in Flims.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Liquidity requirement met via cash accounts, core equity positions left untouched.

lifeEvents.description: Discussion on succession; client requested a strict flag to block any high‑beta speculative asset classes.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reinforced defensive bias and added explicit exclusion rules.

lifeEvents.description: Opened a minor savings account for the oldest grandson, emphasizing traditional saving virtues and avoidance of speculative trading apps.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Further cemented the family’s conservative saving culture.

lifeEvents.description: Account handover protocol initiated due to Thomas Keller’s early retirement.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Ensured continuity of the defensive allocation under a new relationship manager.

lifeEvents.description: Formal reassignment of the account to new RM Sarah Meier.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Maintained the established conservative mandate without disruption.

lifeEvents.description: Requested all upcoming June bond coupons be swept to the family foundation.

personalProfile.interests: Traditional wealth stewardship, family legacy, sustainable farming, fly fishing
personalProfile.quirks: Prefers formal, reserved communication; values quiet, predictable performance; avoids speculative trading apps

personalProfile.avoidTopics: Speculative tech stocks, competitor banks, rival sports teams

keyQuotes: "I want to sleep at night, not speculate on Silicon Valley cloud bubbles."
"These abstract software and AI firms are valued on pure hype. It feels like the dot‑com crash of 2000 all over again."
"We expect the same quiet, global defensive value approach that Thomas Keller provided. No aggressive strategy shifts."
